title: Stomatanthes africanus
taxonomy: Stomatanthes africanus is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to tropical Africa, and its classification falls within the genus Stomatanthes, which is part of the tribe Heliantheae. Further classification places it within the subfamily Asteroideae, a large and diverse group within the Asteraceae family. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its flower structure and leaf shape. The exact classification of Stomatanthes africanus can be further refined through molecular analysis.

intro: Stomatanthes africanus, also known as African Stomat, is a plant species in the Asteraceae family, traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce inflammation, prevent infections, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Stomatanthes africanus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, often used to support immune function and reduce symptoms of respiratory infections.
